title: Phase 9 Capstone Review
concept: The Phase 9 Capstone synthesizes semantic, instance, and panoptic segmentation, FCNs, U-Net, Mask R-CNN, DeepLab, losses, and evaluation metrics.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of Resize-Conv over transposed convolutions?

options.text: It eliminates checkerboard artifacts during upsampling
It reduces the number of classes the model can predict
It runs exclusively on CPU cores to save power

exercises.explain: Correct! Resize-Conv provides smooth upsampling without grid artifacts.

cards.title: The graduation
cards.body: Congratulations! You have navigated the entire landscape of Image Segmentation. From classifying every pixel (semantic) to separating individual objects (instance) and mapping entire scenes (panoptic). You've mastered U-Net, Mask R-CNN, DeepLab, specialized losses, and medical applications. Let's do a final review to lock in your mastery.

cards.title: Segmentation Synthesis
cards.body: Image Segmentation is a cornerstone of computer vision. Whether using FCNs, symmetric U-Nets, multi-branch Mask R-CNNs, or dilated DeepLab models, the goal is always pixel-level understanding. We evaluate using mIoU and Dice, and deploy using edge compression.

cards.title: The master builder
cards.body: You now have a complete toolbox. You don't just know what a hammer is; you know when to use a medical mallet (3D U-Net), a heavy construction hammer (DeepLab v3+), a precision chisel (RoIAlign), or a lightweight multi-tool (MobileNet-FCN).

cards.title: The complete vision developer
cards.body: An AI engineer encounters a new project: segmenting coral reefs. They immediately structure a pipeline: collect expert consensus, select a U-Net with skip connections, train using a Dice + Focal loss, evaluate with mIoU, and quantize to INT8 for subsea drone deployment.

cards.title: Not the end of the road
cards.body: You might think image segmentation is the final frontier of computer vision. In reality, it is the foundation for advanced tasks like 3D scene reconstruction, video tracking, and multi-modal models. The journey continues!

cards.title: The Phase 9 Segmentation Map
cards.body: A summary of everything we've learned:

left.text:
FCN (Ditched dense layers) -> U-Net (Symmetric skips)
Mask R-CNN (Parallel mask branch) -> DeepLab (ASPP & Dilations)

right.text:
Dice & Jaccard Loss (Imbalance proof) -> Focal (Hard boundaries)
Metrics: mIoU (Overlap standard) & Hausdorff (Boundary spikes)

exercises.prompt: Which architecture is characterized by its symmetric U-shape and skip connections that concatenate feature maps?
exercises.explain: Correct! U-Net is defined by its symmetric contraction and expansion paths with concatenating skip connections.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Dice score and F1-score are mathematically identical.
exercises.explain: True. They are different names for the same overlap-based formula.

exercises.sentence: To expand the receptive field without downsampling or adding parameters, we use dilated or '___' convolutions.
exercises.explain: Dilated convolutions are also called atrous convolutions.

exercises.prompt: Which metric evaluates the worst-case boundary alignment error in physical units (like mm)?
exercises.explain: Hausdorff distance measures the maximum contour distance, identifying local spikes.

exercises.prompt: You are asked to design a segmentation model for a robotic surgery arm. It must identify organ boundaries with high safety, handle tiny vessels, and run in real-time. What is your final design blueprint?

options.text: Use an Attention U-Net with a MobileNet backbone, train using a Hybrid Dice + Focal loss, evaluate using mIoU and Hausdorff distance, and apply INT8 quantization for deployment.
Use an FCN-32s with no skip connections, train using standard cross-entropy, and deploy in FP32 format.
Use a basic classification CNN with a dense output layer, trained using mean squared error.
Use standard Mask R-CNN with a heavy ResNet-101 backbone and a CRF post-processor.

exercises.explain: Perfect! This design is safe, handles imbalance (Focal + Dice), preserves fine structures (Attention U-Net), validates safety (mIoU + Hausdorff), and runs in real-time (MobileNet + INT8). You are ready to build production-grade vision AI! Phase 9: Image Segmentation is complete. Next up: Advanced Vision Applications.
